summary: Iteration 6 backend testing complete — 22/22 new iter6 tests pass + full regression. Verified extended profile fields on /auth/register, PATCH /me/profile, PATCH /me/credentials (email & password change), and GET /admin/customers/{id}. All previous iter1-5 tests (backend_test.py 73, test_iteration4.py 22, test_iteration5.py 16) re-run and pass — including test_session_status_has_pairing_fields which was the lone failure flagged in iter5 (main agent has fixed it). Grand total this run: 133/133 tests passing across 4 files.

minor.issue: On email change, server resets auth cookies via set_auth_cookies(response, ...) — good. But the sub-claim of the access JWT still encodes the old email until the next login/refresh. Token decode is by user_id, so this is harmless, just a note.

Server lower-cases new_email before comparison & save (good). However it does NOT enforce min_length on new_email beyond Pydantic EmailStr — empty string-ish payloads like '   @x.com  ' will fail EmailStr; acceptable.

Empty body returns current user with status 200 — matches spec. But `model_dump(exclude_unset=True)` means a client passing {"phone": null} will NOT clear the phone (null is filtered out). If 'unset phone' is a desired UX, the API needs an explicit way to clear (e.g., empty string). Current behaviour matches the iter6 spec, so flagging as code-review only.

Carry-over: this endpoint does not accept profile fields (phone/company/country/city) the way /auth/register does — only name+email+password+quota. If admin needs to create customers with full profile, expand CustomerCreateIn. Not in iter6 scope.

HTTP 200 vs body statusCode=201 on /v2/sendMessage scheduled, past-delay silent fall-through, sentMessages prefix-anchor, url= SSRF, country-code heuristic, dispatcher TTL, default_country_code empty validation — none re-tested here, still pending main agent action.

action_items: None blocking. Iter6 features fully working as specified.
Optional: tighten access-JWT regeneration on email change so the token's email-claim reflects the new email immediately (currently fine because middleware decodes by user_id).
Optional: extend POST /admin/customers (admin create) to accept phone/company/country/city for parity with /auth/register and PATCH /me/profile.
Carry-forward (still pending from iter4/iter5): see iteration_5.json action_items.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py /me/profile (line 522-534): uses model_dump(exclude_unset=True) which is correct. Good. But the strip-on-string also strips non-empty whitespace-only inputs to '' and saves them — consider treating '' as 'do not change' or as explicit clear, but pick one and document.

/app/backend/server.py /me/credentials (line 537-565): correctly verifies current_password BEFORE email/password change, returns 401 'Current password is incorrect'. Email uniqueness check uses {'email': new_email, 'id': {'$ne': user.id}} — correct. Sets new auth cookies — correct. No timing-safe comparison concerns since auth_mod.verify_password uses bcrypt.

/app/backend/server.py /admin/customers/{id} (line 412-425): correctly excludes _id and password_hash from projection. sessions_count/messages_total use count_documents — fine for current scale, but at >100k messages per customer this becomes slow; consider an aggregate or cached counter on the user doc.
RegisterIn (line 73-80): all 4 profile fields are Optional with sensible max_length. Good. No regex validation on phone — international format not enforced (acceptable since server stores raw).
UserOut (line 102-118): adds phone/company/country/city as Optional[str]. user_to_out forwards them via .get(). Good.

server.py is now ~1462 lines (up from 1364 in iter5). Splitting into routers/ subpackage is overdue: auth, sessions, messages, billing, admin, public_v1, plugins, webhooks. Iter4 review already flagged this twice.
test_credentials: admin@wa.9x.design / admin123 — verified working at end of run (NOT changed). All test users prefixed TEST_iter6_*.
seed_data_creation: Each run creates ~9 fresh customer accounts: TEST_iter6_fullprof_*, TEST_iter6_minimal_*, TEST_iter6_wrongpw_*, TEST_iter6_nothing_*, TEST_iter6_chpw_*, TEST_iter6_chmail_* (and its renamed _new), TEST_iter6_dupA_*, TEST_iter6_dupB_*. No sessions/messages created by iter6 file; sessions_count=0 / messages_total=0 verified for the fullprof customer. Existing iter4 seeds (sessions/scheduled/messages) still created by test_iteration4.py.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter6 backend = profile + credentials + admin customer detail. All green. The iter5 known bug (status missing pairing_code/pairing_phone) is now FIXED — confirmed by test_iteration5.py::test_session_status_has_pairing_fields PASSED. Carry-forward minors from iter4 remain unfixed (HTTP 201 schedule, past-delay reject, sentMessages anchor, SSRF on url=, country-code prefix, dispatcher TTL, default_country_code validation). Total suite runtime: ~95s when running all 4 files together (60s scheduler wait in iter4 dominates). Use BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Admin password is admin123 and was not modified. For iter7, add tests for: (a) PATCH /me/profile clearing-by-empty-string semantics if main agent decides to support that, (b) /admin/customers/{id} with sessions_count>0 and messages_total>0 to verify the count aggregation on a populated customer.
rca of the issue: No issues found in iter6 features. All 22 new tests pass on first proper run (3 initial failures were test-side bugs comparing mixed-case emails to backend-lowercased emails — fixed in test by .lower(). Backend behaviour is correct.)
